summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Auto Invoice Generation enhancement. All 10 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified working correctly with 4 stats cards, preview dialog, and proper validation.

issues: Minor: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (console warning)

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/auto-invoice/eligible-companies returns eligible/ineligible companies with reasons
Backend: Eligible companies filter - only includes companies with start_date <= billing period start
Backend: Due date calculation - returns invoice_date + 4 days (verified: 2026-01-01 -> 2026-01-05)
Backend: Ineligible companies show correct reason (start_date after billing OR existing invoice)
Backend: Invalid billing_month format returns 400 error
Backend: Total estimated amount includes 18% GST
Backend: POST /api/auto-invoice/generate returns proper message when no eligible companies
Backend: MongoDB ObjectId properly excluded from response (fixed during testing)
Frontend: Auto Invoice page shows 4 stats cards (Eligible Companies, Ineligible/Skipped, Est. Amount with GST, Due Date)
Frontend: Preview Companies button opens dialog with eligible/ineligible companies
Frontend: Preview dialog shows Invoice Date and Due Date (+4 days) labels
Frontend: Generate button is disabled when no eligible companies (0 eligible)
Frontend: Generate button shows count (e.g., 'Generate 13 Invoices') when companies are eligible
Frontend: Info messages explain start date validation ('respects client start dates')
Frontend: Info messages explain due date calculation ('4 days after invoice issue date')

bugs_fixed_during_testing.issue: Duplicate 'return runs' statement on line 662 after eligible-companies endpoint
MongoDB ObjectId not serializable - insert_one adds _id to result_dict in place
bugs_fixed_during_testing.fix: Added result_dict.pop('_id', None) before returning response

critical_code_review_comments: Start date validation correctly implemented: companies with start_date > billing_date are filtered out
Due date calculation correctly changed from 15th of month to invoice_date + 4 days
Eligible/ineligible companies properly categorized with clear reasons
Frontend stats cards update dynamically when billing month changes
Generate button properly disabled when eligibleCount === 0
seed_data_creation: Used existing companies with various start dates: To Be Honest Circle LLP (2026-01-01), Last 2 Brain Cells (2026-01-06), Apex Legal Eagles (2026-02-01), Golden Rise International (2026-04-01), etc.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto Invoice Generation enhancement fully implemented and tested. Key endpoints: GET /api/auto-invoice/eligible-companies?billing_month=YYYY-MM (returns eligible/ineligible companies with reasons), POST /api/auto-invoice/generate (respects start_date validation, sets due_date to invoice_date + 4 days). Test scenarios: January 2026 = 1 eligible (To Be Honest Circle LLP), April 2026 = 13 eligible, March 2026 = 0 eligible (all have existing invoices). Frontend shows 4 stats cards, preview dialog with company details and reasons, and proper button state management.

eligible_companies_endpoint.returns: billing_month, invoice_date, due_date, eligible_count, ineligible_count, eligible_companies, ineligible_companies, total_estimated_amount

start_date_validation.rule: Companies with start_date > billing_date are marked ineligible
start_date_validation.reason_format: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD) is after billing period (YYYY-MM)

preview_dialog.shows: Eligible count, Ineligible count, Invoice Date, Due Date (+4 days), company tables with reasons
